name: Bourbeuse River
description: A slow, deeply meandering, runoff-fed tributary of the Meramec in east-central Missouri. Low-gradient and turbid — more lowland float than the clear spring-fed Ozark streams. Drops into a low/marginal float range through summer and fall; floatable window skews to spring and after rain. Logjams and low-water bridges are recurring hazards.
managingAuthority: Missouri Department of Conservation (state-managed accesses); streambed navigability per Missouri law

accessLaw.value: Missouri follows the "navigable in fact" doctrine: the public may float and wade streams navigable in fact, but streambeds/banks are frequently privately owned, so access is legal from public road crossings, public land, and established accesses, while portaging or camping on private gravel bars can be trespass. Navigability is decided stream by stream; there is no statewide list. (Shared Missouri paragraph — confirm the AGO/MDC citation once for the state.)
accessLaw.source: https://mdc.mo.gov/ (MDC stream access) — confirm AGO citation once for MO

gauges.name: Bourbeuse River at Union, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified (USGS page 403'd; site #/name cross-checked across ≥2 search results). Long-record lower-river reference at the Hwy 50 bridge; record to 1897; params discharge(00060)+gage height(00065)+precip per USGS listing. Source note: reliable for the entire lower river EXCEPT the Noser Mill→Spring Creek stretch, which runs lower than the rest.

gauges.name: Bourbeuse River above Union, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified (search-surfaced). Just above Union; confirm active + params.

gauges.name: Bourbeuse River near High Gate, MO

sections.name: Lower Bourbeuse (Noser Mill / Union area)
sections.description: The commonly floated lower river represented by the long-record Union gauge (Hwy 50 bridge). Slow, meandering, runoff-fed; summer/fall low water makes riffles scrape or require walking. The Noser Mill→Spring Creek stretch runs lower than the rest of this section.

representativeGauge.rationale: Union (07016500) is the long-record lower-river gauge the floatability guidance is quoted against (cfs)
representativeGauge.knownBias: reads for the lower river; Noser Mill→Spring Creek runs lower than the gauge suggests

thresholds.source: OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse River overview (via WebSearch synthesis, 2026-07): '<40 cfs most riffles will have to be walked' at the Bourbeuse River at Union gauge
OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse River overview (via WebSearch synthesis, 2026-07): '40–70 cfs runnable with considerable scraping'; minimum comfortable ≈70 cfs at the Union gauge
OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse River overview (via WebSearch synthesis, 2026-07): '70–120 cfs low but floatable' at the Union gauge → top of the low-but-floatable band taken as optimal_min

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: Below ~40 cfs at Union most riffles must be walked; 40–70 cfs means considerable bottom-scraping. Normal Jun–Nov flow (60–150 cfs) sits in the low-to-marginal range, so summer floats commonly drag. (SEARCH-DERIVED, verify.)
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Runoff-fed and turbid; rises after rain. Logjams/strainers and low-water bridges become the practical hazards as water comes up. NWS UNNM7 flood/action stages auto-fill at ingest.

localKnowledge: Runoff-fed, not spring-fed: turbid and slower than the clear Ozark floats; drops into a marginal float range through summer/fall (OzarkAnglers, via search).
Extreme meandering — high river-miles per straight-line mile; plan float times accordingly.
